## Pattern Assignments

### `src/ai/awsCredentials.ts` (service, request-response — REWRITE)

**Analog:** `src/ai/awsCredentials.ts` (current Phase 08.1 — 138 LoC)

**nodeRequire shim pattern** (lines 34-49 — UNCHANGED, stays in place):
```typescript
function nodeRequire<T>(id: string): T | undefined {
  const g = activeWindow as unknown as {
    require?: CjsRequire;
    module?: { require?: CjsRequire };
  };
  const fn =
    g.require ??
    g.module?.require ??
    (typeof __webpack_require__ === 'function' ? __webpack_require__ : undefined);
  if (!fn) return undefined;
  try {
    return fn(id) as T;
  } catch {
    return undefined;
  }
}
```

**Public entry signature** (lines 68-72 — preserved verbatim for backward compat):
```typescript
export function resolveAwsCredentials(
  opts:
    | { source: 'env-or-default-profile' }
    | { source: 'profile'; profile: string },
): { accessKeyId?: string; secretAccessKey?: string; sessionToken?: string } {
```

**Env-var read guard** (lines 74-86 — reuse pattern for `process.env` access):
```typescript
  if (opts.source === 'env-or-default-profile') {
    const e = (typeof process !== 'undefined' ? process.env : {}) as Record<
      string,
      string | undefined
    >;
    if (e.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID && e.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) {
      return {
        accessKeyId: e.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID,
        secretAccessKey: e.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY,
        sessionToken: e.AWS_SESSION_TOKEN,
      };
    }
  }
```

**INI parser pattern** (lines 111-138 — extended to also extract `credential_process` and `region` keys):
```typescript
export function parseIniProfile(
  text: string,
  profile: string,
): { accessKeyId?: string; secretAccessKey?: string; sessionToken?: string } {
  const lines = text.split(/\r?\n/);
  let inSection = false;
  const out: Record<string, string> = {};
  for (const line of lines) {
    const trimmed = line.trim();
    if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('#') || trimmed.startsWith(';')) continue;
    if (trimmed.startsWith('[') && trimmed.endsWith(']')) {
      const name = trimmed.slice(1, -1).trim();
      inSection = name === profile || name === `profile ${profile}`;
      continue;
    }
    if (inSection) {
      const eq = trimmed.indexOf('=');
      if (eq > 0) {
        out[trimmed.slice(0, eq).trim()] = trimmed.slice(eq + 1).trim();
      }
    }
  }
  return {
    accessKeyId: out.aws_access_key_id,
    secretAccessKey: out.aws_secret_access_key,
    sessionToken: out.aws_session_token,
  };
}
```

**Error collapse pattern** (lines 93-99 — empty-object collapse on any file read failure):
```typescript
  try {
    const credPath = path.join(os.homedir(), '.aws', 'credentials');
    const text = fs.readFileSync(credPath, 'utf8');
    // ...
  } catch {
    return {};
  }
```

### `src/ai/credentialProcess.ts` (new service, request-response + cache)

**Analog:** `src/ai/awsCredentials.ts` (nodeRequire shim) + `src/ai/providers/index.ts:105-109` (extractProviderError error shape)

**nodeRequire for child_process** — same shim from `awsCredentials.ts:34-49`. Either import from sibling or duplicate the private helper:
```typescript
// Reuse verbatim — same pattern, different module id
const cp = nodeRequire<ChildProcessLike>('child_process');
if (!cp) {
  throw new Error('credential_process: child_process unavailable from renderer');
}
```

**Error message construction** — copy the 200-char truncation pattern from `extractProviderError` (lines 98, 105-109 of `src/ai/providers/index.ts`):
```typescript
/** Best-effort message extraction from any thrown value. */
export function extractProviderError(err: unknown): string {
  if (err instanceof Error) return err.message;
  if (typeof err === 'string') return err;
  return 'Unknown error';
}
```

Applied to credential_process errors — truncate stderr at source (D-08):
```typescript
const stderrTruncated = (result.stderr ?? '').slice(0, 200);
if (result.status !== 0) {
  throw new Error(`credential_process exited ${result.status}: ${stderrTruncated}`);
}
```

**Module-level cache pattern** — discriminated union (no existing analog in codebase; RESEARCH.md Pattern 6 is the reference):
```typescript
interface FreshEntry {
  status: 'fresh';
  creds: ResolvedCredentials;
  expiresAt: number;
}
interface RefreshingEntry {
  status: 'refreshing';
  promise: Promise<ResolvedCredentials>;
}
type CacheEntry = FreshEntry | RefreshingEntry;

const SKEW_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;
const cache = new Map<string, CacheEntry>();
```

### `src/ai/providers/bedrock.ts` (adapter, request-response — MODIFY)

**Analog:** `src/ai/providers/bedrock.ts` (self — lines 51-75)

**sessionToken passthrough in access-keys branch** (line 56-59 — add `opts.sessionToken`):
```typescript
    case 'access-keys': {
      opts.accessKeyId = cfg.accessKeyId;
      opts.secretAccessKey = cfg.secretAccessKey;
      // NEW: D-03 sessionToken passthrough for temporary STS credentials
      opts.sessionToken = cfg.sessionToken;
      break;
    }
```

**probeBedrock error handling** (lines 90-99 — truncation at the SDK layer; unchanged contract):
```typescript
export async function probeBedrock(
  cfg: BedrockProviderConfig,
  fetcher: FetchFn,
): Promise<ProbeResult> {
  try {
    await generateText({
      model: createBedrockModel(cfg, fetcher),
      prompt: 'ping',
      maxOutputTokens: 1,
    });
    return { ok: true, modelCount: null };
  } catch (err) {
    return { ok: false, errorMessage: extractProviderError(err).slice(0, 200) };
  }
}
```

### `src/ai/types.ts` (model — MODIFY)

**Analog:** `src/ai/types.ts` (self — lines 57-73)

**Field addition pattern** — follow existing optional field style with JSDoc:
```typescript
export interface BedrockProviderConfig extends ProviderConfig {
  region: string;
  modelId: string;
  authMethod: 'default-chain' | 'access-keys' | 'sso-profile' | 'api-key';
  accessKeyId?: string;
  secretAccessKey?: string;
  ssoProfile?: string;
  bedrockApiKey?: string;
  /** Set when authMethod === 'access-keys'. Session token for temporary STS credentials. Preserved across mode switches. */
  sessionToken?: string;  // NEW: D-03
}
```

### `src/settings/SettingsStore.ts` (config, CRUD — MODIFY)

**Analog:** `src/settings/SettingsStore.ts:464-499` (`sanitizeBedrockProviderConfig`)

**Shape-guard field addition pattern** (lines 493-497 — follow the existing field-preservation style):
```typescript
    // All 4 secret fields preserved verbatim regardless of authMethod
    // (Pitfall 10 — mode switch must not clear inactive-mode secrets).
    accessKeyId: typeof r.accessKeyId === 'string' ? r.accessKeyId : '',
    secretAccessKey: typeof r.secretAccessKey === 'string' ? r.secretAccessKey : '',
    ssoProfile: typeof r.ssoProfile === 'string' ? r.ssoProfile : '',
    bedrockApiKey: typeof r.bedrockApiKey === 'string' ? r.bedrockApiKey : '',
    // NEW: D-03 — sessionToken field (same preserve-on-mode-switch invariant)
    sessionToken: typeof r.sessionToken === 'string' ? r.sessionToken : '',
```


**Conditional secret input pattern** (lines 472-496 — add sessionToken under access-keys):
```typescript
        } else if (bcfg.authMethod === 'access-keys') {
          // Existing: Access key ID input
          new Setting(containerEl)
            .setName('Access key ID')
            .setDesc('AWS access key ID. Stored in plain text in data.json on this machine.')
            .addText((t) => {
              t.inputEl.type = 'password';
              t.inputEl.addClass('lc-ai-input');
              t.setValue(bcfg.accessKeyId ?? '');
              t.onChange(async (v) => {
                const current = this.plugin.settings.getProviderConfig(active) as BedrockProviderConfig;
                await this.plugin.settings.setProviderConfig(active, { ...current, accessKeyId: v });
              });
            });
          // ... existing secretAccessKey input ...
          // NEW: D-03 sessionToken input (same structure as secretAccessKey)
          new Setting(containerEl)
            .setName('Session token')
            .setDesc('Required only for temporary STS credentials.')
            .addText((t) => {
              t.inputEl.type = 'password';
              t.inputEl.addClass('lc-ai-input');
              t.setValue(bcfg.sessionToken ?? '');
              t.onChange(async (v) => {
                const current = this.plugin.settings.getProviderConfig(active) as BedrockProviderConfig;
                await this.plugin.settings.setProviderConfig(active, { ...current, sessionToken: v });
              });
            });
```

### `tests/ai/credentialProcess.test.ts` (new test)

**Analog:** `tests/ai/awsCredentials.test.ts` (entire file — 215 lines)

**Test harness setup** (lines 10-56 — module reset + global stub + env cleanup):
```typescript
import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest';

const ORIGINAL_ENV = { ...process.env };

function clearAwsEnv(): void {
  delete process.env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID;
  delete process.env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY;
  delete process.env.AWS_SESSION_TOKEN;
}

beforeEach(() => {
  vi.resetModules();
  vi.unstubAllGlobals();
  clearAwsEnv();
});

afterEach(() => {
  vi.unstubAllGlobals();
  process.env = { ...ORIGINAL_ENV };
});
```

**stubNodeModules pattern** (lines 18-42 — fake require with fs/path/os/child_process):
```typescript
function stubNodeModules(opts: { credentialsContent?: string; throwOnRead?: Error }): void {
  const fakeFs: FakeFs = {
    readFileSync(_p: string, _e: string): string {
      if (opts.throwOnRead) throw opts.throwOnRead;
      return opts.credentialsContent ?? '';
    },
  };
  const fakePath = {
    join(...parts: string[]): string {
      return parts.join('/');
    },
  };
  const fakeOs = {
    homedir(): string {
      return '/home/testuser';
    },
  };
  const fakeRequire = vi.fn((id: string) => {
    if (id === 'fs') return fakeFs;
    if (id === 'path') return fakePath;
    if (id === 'os') return fakeOs;
    throw new Error(`unexpected require: ${id}`);
  });
  vi.stubGlobal('activeWindow', { require: fakeRequire });
}
```

**Dynamic import pattern for module isolation** (lines 67-73):
```typescript
  it('returns env vars when set', async () => {
    process.env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID = 'AKIAEXAMPLE';
    // ...
    const { resolveAwsCredentials } = await import('../../src/ai/awsCredentials');
    const creds = resolveAwsCredentials({ source: 'env-or-default-profile' });
    expect(creds.accessKeyId).toBe('AKIAEXAMPLE');
  });
```

## Shared Patterns

### nodeRequire Shim (S2 from Phase 08.1)
**Source:** `src/ai/awsCredentials.ts:34-49`
**Apply to:** `src/ai/credentialProcess.ts` (for `child_process` import)
```typescript
function nodeRequire<T>(id: string): T | undefined {
  const g = activeWindow as unknown as {
    require?: CjsRequire;
    module?: { require?: CjsRequire };
  };
  const fn =
    g.require ??
    g.module?.require ??
    (typeof __webpack_require__ === 'function' ? __webpack_require__ : undefined);
  if (!fn) return undefined;
  try {
    return fn(id) as T;
  } catch {
    return undefined;
  }
}
```

### Error Truncation (200-char cap at source)
**Source:** `src/ai/providers/index.ts:98` (body.slice(0, 200)) and `src/ai/providers/bedrock.ts:98` (extractProviderError(err).slice(0, 200))
**Apply to:** `src/ai/credentialProcess.ts` — stderr truncation at the awsCredentials boundary (D-08)
```typescript
const stderrTruncated = (result.stderr ?? '').slice(0, 200);
```

### Empty-Object Collapse (never throw from file reads)
**Source:** `src/ai/awsCredentials.ts:91-99`
**Apply to:** All file-read paths in the rewritten `awsCredentials.ts` (safeReadFile helper)
```typescript
  try {
    return fs.readFileSync(p, 'utf8');
  } catch {
    return '';  // D-18: file-absence collapses, never throws
  }
```

### Settings Shape-Guard Field Pattern
**Source:** `src/settings/SettingsStore.ts:493-497`
**Apply to:** New `sessionToken` field in `sanitizeBedrockProviderConfig`
```typescript
    fieldName: typeof r.fieldName === 'string' ? r.fieldName : '',
```

### Test Harness: vi.resetModules + dynamic import
**Source:** `tests/ai/awsCredentials.test.ts:52-62`
**Apply to:** `tests/ai/credentialProcess.test.ts` and any extensions to `tests/ai/awsCredentials.test.ts`
```typescript
beforeEach(() => {
  vi.resetModules();
  vi.unstubAllGlobals();
  clearAwsEnv();
});
afterEach(() => {
  vi.unstubAllGlobals();
  process.env = { ...ORIGINAL_ENV };
});
```

### Probe-Never-Throws Contract
**Source:** `src/ai/providers/bedrock.ts:86-99`
**Apply to:** credential_process errors that bubble up through `createBedrockModel` into `probeBedrock` — the try/catch in probeBedrock collapses all errors to `{ ok: false, errorMessage }`. No changes needed at the probe layer.

## No Analog Found

| File | Role | Data Flow | Reason |
|------|------|-----------|--------|
| `src/ai/credentialProcess.ts` (parseCommandLine) | utility | transform | No shell-style command parser exists in the codebase. Use RESEARCH.md Pattern 3 (30-LoC double-quote-aware tokenizer). |
| `src/ai/credentialProcess.ts` (cache + coalescing) | service | cache | No Promise-coalescing cache exists in the codebase. Use RESEARCH.md Pattern 6 (discriminated-union Map). |

**Note:** Both "no analog" components have complete code shapes in RESEARCH.md Patterns 3 and 6 — the planner should reference those directly.
